problem text: Consider $X, Y, Z \sim \text{Unif}(0,1)$, which are independent and identically distributed. Determine the probability that it is possible to create a triangle with sides $X, Y,$ and $Z$.
problem hint: To form an invalid triangle, at least one of these must be true: $X > Y + Z$, $Y > X + Z$, or $Z > X + Y$. Notice that these three scenarios are mutually exclusive. Can you see why?
